zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Lanphear - Lanphere於1594年出生在英國時，他的父親Thomas Thakere Lanphere44歲，母親Ellyn Munning42歲。他於1639年10月27日與Averina Vibert在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓 聖 邁克爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1665年8月21日在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特享年71歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可特查阿培爾。

nameMeanings.name: Lanphear
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of   Lanfear  .

variants.name: Lanfear
meanings: Welsh and English: habitational name from any of the many places in Wales called Llanfair ‘church of Saint Mary’, from Welsh llan ‘church’ + Mair ‘Mary’, for example Llanfair Caereinion (Montgomeryshire). Compare Lamphear , Lamphere , Lamphier , Lanfair , Lanphear , Lanpher , Lanphere , and Lanphier .

Compare Lamphear , Lamphere , Lamphier , Lanfair , Lanphear , Lanpher , Lanphere , and Lanphier .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Lanphear - Lanphere was born in 1594, in England, his father, Thomas Thakere Lanphere, was 44 and his mother, Ellyn Munning, was 42. He married Averina Vibert on 27 October 1639, in Southampton St Michael, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 21 August 1665, in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71, and was buried in Whitechapel, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Lanphear - Lanphere est né en 1594, à Angleterre, son père, Thomas Thakere Lanphere, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Ellyn Munning, avait 42 ans. Il a épousé Averina Vibert le 27 octobre 1639, à Southampton St Michael, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 21 août 1665, à Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Whitechapel, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Lanphear - Lanphereが1594年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Thomas Thakere Lanphereは44歳，母Ellyn Munningは42歳であった。1639年10月27日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトン スト ミチャエルでAverina Vibertと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1665年8月21日にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターで71歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウィテチャペルにて埋葬された。

ko.Southampton St Michaels, Hampshire, Engl: 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴 스트 믹하얼
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Lanphear - Lanphere(이)가 1594년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Thakere Lanphere(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellyn Munning(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1639년 10월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴 스트 믹하얼에서 Averina Vibert(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1665년 8월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 휘테차플에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Lanphear - Lanphere nasceu em 1594, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Thakere Lanphere, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Ellyn Munning, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Averina Vibert em 27 de outubro de 1639, em Southampton St Michael, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.Southampton St Michaels, Hampshire, Engl: соутамптон ст мачаел, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Lanphear - Lanphere родился в 1594 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Thakere Lanphere было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Ellyn Munning, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Averina Vibert 27 октября 1639 г. в местности соутамптон ст мачаел, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 21 августа 1665 г. в местности вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности уитечапел, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.
